programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain
programme.desc: How the great icons of British Gothic architecture were all created by one family.

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: Two unlikely partners in a bounty-hunting scam join forces in a new venture. Contains some violence and some upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Clint Eastwood: The Man with No Name
programme.desc: A profile of actor and director Clint Eastwood in which he talks to Iain Johnstone. Deals with adult themes.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah examines the skill of the shipbuilders and machine engineers who turned Britain into a great manufacturing nation.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores London, the ultimate 'city of villages', including a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow.

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain
programme.desc: How the great icons of British Gothic architecture were all created by one family.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 10 September 1999 and featuring Martine McCutcheon, Shania Twain, A1, The Offspring, DJ Jean and Lou Bega.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Emma Ledden presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 17 September 1999 and featuring Supergrass, Thunderbugs, The All Seeing I feat Phil Oakey, Suede and Vengaboys.

programme.title: TOTP: 1983
programme.desc: Richard Skinner and Janice Long present the pop chart programme. Featuring Culture Club, The Human League, FR David, Heaven 17, Thompson Twins, Spandau Ballet and David Bowie.

programme.title: TOTP: 1994
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 21 April 1994 and featuring Crash Test Dummies, Bitty McLean, C. J. Lewis, The Pretenders, Crystal Waters, Deacon Blue and Prince.

programme.title: Prince and His Songs at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of hits from one of pop music's greatest geniuses - Prince, aka 'The Artist Formerly Known as Prince', aka 'the Unpronounceable Symbol', and more.

programme.title: Prince and The Revolution: Live
programme.desc: The Syracuse Carrier Dome show from Prince's 1985 Purple Rain tour. This Grammy-nominated concert film has been entirely remixed, remastered and visually enhanced.

programme.title: Prince: A Purple Reign
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Prince - showman, artist, enigma - revolutionised the perception of black music in the 1980s by embarking on an amazing journey of musical self-discovery.

programme.title: Word Up! Black American Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances by African-American artists of the 1980s from the BBC archives, featuring Cameo, Shalamar, Salt-n-Pepa, Chaka Khan, Kid Creole, Whitney Houston and Luther Vandross.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 10 September 1999 and featuring Martine McCutcheon, Shania Twain, A1, The Offspring, DJ Jean and Lou Bega.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Emma Ledden presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 17 September 1999 and featuring Supergrass, Thunderbugs, The All Seeing I feat Phil Oakey, Suede and Vengaboys.

programme.title: TOTP: 1983
programme.desc: Richard Skinner and Janice Long present the pop chart programme. Featuring Culture Club, The Human League, FR David, Heaven 17, Thompson Twins, Spandau Ballet and David Bowie.

programme.title: TOTP: 1994
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 21 April 1994 and featuring Crash Test Dummies, Bitty McLean, C. J. Lewis, The Pretenders, Crystal Waters, Deacon Blue and Prince.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Catrin Heledd introduces live coverage from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where defending champion Zhao Xintong plays his first-round match to a conclusion.
